t_ch: 我读到过一些骇人的故事，声称「清洗者」会将记忆残片以炼金之术强加给继任者，以此延续「身份」和「仇恨」……
t_en: I've read some chilling accounts, stories of the "Cleaners" using alchemical arts to implant fragments of memories into their successors, ensuring both identity and hatred live on.

t_ch: 据我所知，此乃事实。我们所认识的那位凯妮斯议员，严格来说——其实是历史上第二十七位「凯妮斯」。
t_en: From what I know, these stories are true. The Caenis we know today — strictly speaking — is the <unbreak>27</unbreak>th Caenis.

t_ch: 客观而论，「清洗者」的出现是必然，亦是必须。但他们凶险恶毒的行事手段为众人所不齿，因而落得了被史书唾弃的下场。
t_en: Objectively, the rise of the Cleaners was both inevitable and necessary. Yet, their ruthless and twisted methods earned them nothing but disdain and condemnation from history.

t_ch: 但他们还是作为一个组织延续至今，甚至还在设法阻挠逐火。
t_en: And still, they endure. An organization persisting through time, now working tirelessly to obstruct the Flame-Chase.

t_ch: 他们就那么畏惧黄金裔追求的「再创世」？
t_en: Are they truly so terrified of the "Era Nova" the Chrysos Heirs strive for?

t_ch: 即便身处飘摇末世，也总有人能从困局中获益。
t_en: Even in these crumbling end times, there are always those who find ways to profit from the chaos.

t_ch: 要让既得利益者为遥远的理想抛弃此世的地位、财富、野心——那或许不比缔造奇迹容易。
t_en: To ask the vested interests to abandon their status, wealth, and ambitions in this world for a distant ideal, may be no easier than creating a miracle.

t_ch: …不知{NICKNAME}现在在哪。如果我被他们盯上了，那{F#她}{M#他}肯定也逃不过这遭。希望一切安好……
t_en: ...I wonder where {NICKNAME} is now. If they're targeting me, {F#s}he definitely won't escape their notice either. I hope all is well...

t_ch: 那是…克拉特鲁斯阁下？
t_en: Is that... Lord Krateros?

t_ch: 总算找到你了，丹恒阁下。传信石版联系不上，圣城卫士险些以为你和{NICKNAME}已经遭遇了不测。
t_en: There you are, Lord Dan Heng. We couldn't reach you on the teleslate, so the Holy City Guard almost assumed you and {NICKNAME} had met some terrible fate.

t_ch: 在阴影中爬行的虫豸们，退下吧！阴谋到此为止了。
t_en: You pests crawling in the shadows, back off! Your plot ends here.

t_ch: 你们如愿施行了毒计，趁早四散奔逃吧，兴许你们还能争取到一条活命……
t_en: You've played your hand. Run while you still can. It might be your only chance to survive.

t_ch: 但我奉劝你们永远别再靠近奥赫玛，否则，愤怒的民众将把你们撕成碎片。
t_en: Stay away from Okhema forever. If you set foot there again, the fury of its people will tear you apart.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>因为阿格莱雅死了——而在圣城公民眼里，你们就是杀害她的凶手！</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Because Aglaea is dead, and in the eyes of the holy city's citizens, her blood is on your hands!</color>
Hehe...

t_ch: 看来她对你的判断有所偏误。
t_en: Looks like she was wrong about you.

t_ch: 她…？是在说你们的幕后主使么？
t_en: She...? Are you talking about the mastermind behind all of this?

t_ch: 她认为你很软弱。她认为，哪怕在对战心中坚信的「恶」时，你在挥剑时仍会有犹疑。
t_en: She thought you were weak. She believed that even when you are facing the "evil" that you claim to oppose, you'd hesitate to strike.

t_ch: 会将仁慈误解成软弱，看来你们那位主子自负又愚蠢。
t_en: Misinterpreting mercy for weakness shows how arrogant and foolish your master is.

t_ch: 他们几个都还有气息。稍加照顾，几天以后应该就能恢复。
t_en: They're still alive. With proper care, they'll pull through in a few days.

t_ch: 但我必须警告你——我已经太久没把剑尖指向人类了，没法保证自己能控制好挥剑的力度。他们能活下来算是运气好……
t_en: But I must warn you — I haven't fought a human in ages. I can't guarantee I'll always be able to hold back. They're lucky they're still alive...

t_ch: 你能否和他们一般幸运…想试试吗？
t_en: If you feel as lucky as they are, try me.

t_ch: 伙计，有点帅。
t_en: Bro, you sound so cool.

t_ch: 伙计，有点吓人。
t_en: Bro, you sound kind of scary.

t_ch: 白厄，他真的生气了……
t_en: Phainon is seriously upset...

t_ch: 滚吧，别再出现在我们面前。转告你们身后的主子，想阻止逐火之旅，她最好集结一支比鼎盛时的悬锋更雄壮的军队。
t_en: Get out of here. Don't show your face around us again. If your master wants to stop the Flame-Chase Journey, she'd better bring an army that makes Kremnos at its peak look like child's play.

t_ch: 我不止一次强调过，白厄阁下…我们不为争端而来。
t_en: I've said it more than once, Lord Phainon... we're not here to start a fight.

t_ch: 我们会来这里，<color=#dbc291ff>只是为了保证你们不在别处。</color>
t_en: We came only <color=#dbc291ff>to ensure you weren't anywhere else.</color>

t_ch: 不知不觉间，我们已经达成了「拖延时间」的任务……
t_en: While you were unaware, we already accomplished our mission — to buy time.

t_ch: 那么，白厄阁下…有缘再会。
t_en: So, Lord Phainon... until next time.

t_ch: 你还好吗？
t_en: Are you okay?

t_ch: 我没事。那些家伙竟敢拿公民的性命作为要挟…那就触碰到了底线。
t_en: I'm fine. Anyone who uses innocent lives as bargaining chips has gone too far.

t_ch: 但他们其实是冲着黄金裔来的。
t_en: But they were after the Chrysos Heirs.

t_ch: 嗯。我猜…元老院的反对派还在做绝望的挣扎，想阻挠逐火。
t_en: Hmm. I guess, the opposition within the Council of Elders is still desperately fighting to obstruct the Flame-Chase.

t_ch: 他们真觉得自己有胜算？
t_en: Do they think they have a chance?

t_ch: 恐怕也是孤注一掷…我猜，元老院的反对派还在做绝望的挣扎，想阻挠逐火。
t_en: Probably a desperate gamble... I guess, the opposition within the Council of Elders is still desperately fighting to obstruct the Flame-Chase Journey.

t_ch: 如果阴谋败露，他们在圣城将如过街狗鼠，人人喊打。哪怕再擅长隐匿行踪……
t_en: If the conspiracy is exposed, they'll be hunted down in the holy city like rats with nowhere to run. No matter where they hide...

t_ch: …传信石版？
t_en: ...My teleslate?

t_ch: 阿格莱雅？！这是——
t_en: Aglaea!? Is this —

t_ch: 该死！那些家伙……
t_en: Blast it! Those guys...

t_ch: 骗人的吧…
t_en: It must be a lie...

t_ch: 不止一个人给我传信，这不像玩笑……
t_en: I've heard from more than one person. It doesn't seem like a joke.

t_ch: 是那些家伙干的么？
t_en: Was it those guys' doing?

t_ch: 但只凭他们，怎么可能……
t_en: But how could they possibly...

t_ch: 冷静…这也可能是她的什么计策。直到亲眼确认之前……
t_en: Stay calm... this could just be one of her schemes. I won't believe it until I see it for myself...

t_ch: 白厄……
t_en: Phainon...

t_ch: 我们…现在该怎么办？
t_en: What should we do now?

t_ch: 一切照常。我先给缇宝老师传信，让她尽力安抚公民。我们快马加鞭赶回奥赫玛，去确认城里的状况。假如中途再撞见拦路的敌人……
t_en: Stay on course. I'll contact Tribbie and ask her to keep the citizens calm. We'll hurry back to Okhema as fast as possible and assess the situation in the city. If we run into any enemies along the way...

t_ch: …我不会再心慈手软。
t_en: ...I won't be merciful this time.

t_ch: 「…哈托努斯。」
t_en: "...Chartonus."
Mmm...?

t_ch: 「你在为白厄铸剑，对么？」
t_en: "You're forging a sword for Phainon, aren't you?"

t_ch: 「我已经太久没有目睹过你挥锤淬火的身姿了，刚刚竟看得有些入神。」
t_en: "I haven't seen you wield the hammer in so long... I couldn't help but be entranced by the sight."

t_ch: 你们…阿格莱雅。
t_en: You... Aglaea.

t_ch: 与我相见，以这幅模样。这意味着……
t_en: In this form, you come to me. It means...

t_ch: 「没错。<color=#dbc291ff>此时此刻，我的身形已经破碎。在这世间，我作为『人』的旅程已经来到了终点。</color>」
t_en: "That's right. <color=#dbc291ff>At this moment, my physical form is already shattered. My journey as a human in this world has come to an end.</color>"

t_ch: 「我们此前的约定，现在到了兑现它的时候了。」
t_en: "It's time to fulfill our pact."

t_ch: 约定，当然…我牢记在心。
t_en: Of course... The pact, I haven't forgotten.

t_ch: 「我托付给你的那样东西，它还在吗？」
t_en: "The thing I entrusted to you, do you still have it?"

t_ch: 过于简陋，容器…配不上你的品位。你坚持如此？
t_en: Far too plain, this vessel is... Not worthy of you. Do you still insist?

t_ch: 「我坚持。」
t_en: "I insist."

t_ch: 「过去千年，我手中的所有织物都沾染上了这冰冷的神性。因而，我开始厌恶它们的色彩、触感和气味。」
t_en: "Over the past thousand years, every piece of fabric I've worked with has been tainted by my cold divinity. It's made me hate their colors, texture, and scent."

t_ch: 「但这样饰品不同…它是一个孩子赠予我的礼物。她纯真，善良——我多希望自己的童年能活成她的模样。」
t_en: "This ornament stands apart... It was a gift from a child. She was pure and kind. I often wished my childhood could have been like hers."

t_ch: 和众人告别…你已计划周全？
t_en: How to say goodbye to everyone... have you already planned?

t_ch: 「恐怕这次没有那么周全的计划了。无法和他们一一道别，我倍感遗憾。」
t_en: "I'm afraid there's no such detailed plan this time. I deeply regret not being able to say farewell to them one by one."

t_ch: 「但最好的时机已经来到…我的退场将为他们肃清后顾之忧。这件临别赠礼，应该多少能够弥补这仓促一别的遗憾吧？」
t_en: "The moment has come... My departure will give them one less thing to worry about. This parting gift should help ease the regret of such a sudden goodbye."

t_ch: 针对泰坦发明，悬锋的铸魂技术。虽能保留神性…但会彻底过滤，人性的残余。
t_en: Invented for the Titans, Kremnos's Soul-Forging technology was. Though divinity it retains, filtered out, the remnants of humanity will be.

t_ch: 这是你想要的，阿格莱雅？
t_en: Is that what you want, Aglaea?

t_ch: 「你已经与我确认再三了。没错，哈托努斯，我需要你为我这么做。」
t_en: "You've confirmed this with me repeatedly. Yes, Chartonus, I need you to do this for me."

t_ch: 「将我的神性凝聚起来，注入这件饰品…或许它还能在未来派上用场，帮助他们完成使命。」
t_en: "Condense my divinity into this ornament... It might still be useful in the future to help them complete their mission."

t_ch: 无私，直到最后。
t_en: Selfless, until the end.

t_ch: 那孩童，不必羡慕…你的人性，已走过属于它的，伟大一生。
t_en: Envy that child, there's no need to... Already lived a grand life, your humanity has.

t_ch: 「呵…你的赞赏令我百般安心，大工匠。现在……」
t_en: "Heh... your praise puts my heart at ease, Grand Craftsman. Now..."

t_ch: 「…请带我经历命运的最后一次淬炼吧。」
t_en: "...please take me through the last tempering that fate has to offer."

t_ch: 城中一片寂静，不安的情绪分秒都在累积。
t_en: The city is silent, and tension is building with every passing second.

t_ch: 消息恐怕已经传开了。家家户户都闭门不出…市集的所有店铺都临时关张。
t_en: The news has probably spread by now. Every household is locked up tight... and all the market stalls have closed down temporarily.

t_ch: 她真的是被清洗者…
t_en: She really was taken by the Cleaners...

t_ch: 难以置信，她怎么可能被区区几个刺客……
t_en: I can't imagine, how she could've been taken down by a handful of assassins...

t_ch: 除非也是她计谋的一环。
t_en: Unless it's all a part of her plan.

t_ch: 总觉得这是她的计谋…
t_en: Something about this feels like a scheme...

t_ch: 我希望如此……
t_en: I hope so...

t_ch: 无论如何我都无法相信，她会被区区几个刺客……
t_en: I just can't believe she'd be taken down by a handful of assassins...

t_ch: 如果这一切都是计策，那缇宝老师一定知道些什么，只是不便在消息中明说。
t_en: If this is all part of a larger plan, Tribbie must know something. She just couldn't say it in the message.

t_ch: 走吧，我们去找她。
t_en: Let's go find her.

t_ch: 啊，你们回来了。状况如何，没受伤吧？
t_en: Ah, you're back. How's everything? No injuries, I hope?

t_ch: 放心，清洗者没伤到我们。
t_en: Don't worry. The Cleaners didn't lay a hand on us.

t_ch: 克拉特鲁斯阁下，事发时你在城里？究竟发生了什么……
t_en: Lord Krateros, were you in the city when it happened? What exactly went down...

t_ch: 很遗憾，没人知道当时的详细状况。
t_en: Regrettably, no one knows the details of that moment.

t_ch: 目击者只看到了…唔……
t_en: Eyewitnesses only saw... um...

t_ch: …没关系，请直说吧。
t_en: ...It's fine. Just tell me.

t_ch: …云石天宫的目击者看到阿格莱雅从黄金裔浴池坠落。
t_en: ...Eyewitnesses at the Marmoreal Palace saw Aglaea fall from the Chrysos Heir Bath.

t_ch: 天啊……
t_en: Oh gods...

t_ch: 圣城卫士上前查看时，她已经没有生命迹象了。
t_en: She showed no signs of life by the time the Holy City Guards approached to check.

t_ch: 但他们甚至不敢妄下定论…因为她是活过千年的半神，黄金裔的领袖。这城中的大部分公民都以为她与死亡二字无关。
t_en: They were too afraid to jump to conclusions... After all, she was a demigod who'd lived for over a thousand years, the leader of the Chrysos Heirs. Most citizens of this city believed that death couldn't touch her.

t_ch: 她被一把制式奇特的匕首刺穿了心脏。我亲眼看到以后才确信，那是「清洗者」佩戴的暗器。
t_en: She was pierced through the heart by a uniquely crafted dagger. I saw it for myself. It's one of the weapons carried by the "Cleaners."

t_ch: 那他们为什么没对我们下手？
t_en: Why didn't they go after us?

t_ch: 我和你想的一样…这完全不合逻辑。
t_en: I also don't understand... This doesn't add up.

t_ch: 所以她真的是被刺杀的…
t_en: She really was assassinated...

t_ch: 不，不对……
t_en: No, that doesn't make sense...

t_ch: 在树庭出现的家伙，还有在重渊和丹恒对峙的那些，都没有立刻诉诸暴力，只是监视和威慑。
t_en: The ones who appeared in the Grove, and those who confronted Dan Heng in the Abyss, didn't immediately resort to violence. They were just watching and trying to intimidate.

t_ch: 他们没理由，也没那个胆子对阿格莱雅动武。难道……
t_en: They didn't have any reason, nor the courage, to attack Aglaea. Perhaps...

t_ch: 径自臆测意义不大。去找缇宝女士吧，她最懂得阿格莱雅的心思。
t_en: Speculating blindly won't help. Let's go find Lady Tribbie, as she understands Aglaea's mind best.

t_ch: 我会留在这里，带领卫士维持治安。你们就去弄清这起事情的原委吧——但别花太久，阿格莱雅已逝，必须有人代替她引领奥赫玛。
t_en: I'll stay here to lead the guards and maintain order. Find out what happened — but don't take too long. With Aglaea gone, someone has to take her place and lead Okhema.

t_ch: 对了，风堇姑娘…她作为医师宣告了阿格莱雅的死亡。你们或许也该和她聊聊。
t_en: Also, Miss Hyacine was the one who declared Aglaea dead. You might want to speak with her.

t_ch: …感谢你，克拉特鲁斯阁下。
t_en: ...Thank you, Lord Krateros.

t_ch: 风堇。
t_en: Hyacine.

t_ch: …你们回来了！
t_en: ...You're back!

t_ch: 我们听克拉特鲁斯说了。你…还好么？
t_en: We heard from Krateros. Are you... alright?

t_ch: 我没事…事发以后，我就一直陪在缇宝和缇宁老师身边。
t_en: I'm fine... I've been staying close to Lady Tribbie and Lady Trinnon after the incident.

t_ch: 你们能这么快赶回来真是太好了…她们现在很需要有人陪伴。
t_en: It's great that you could come back so quickly... They need someone with them now.

t_ch: 她们两个状态如何？
t_en: How are the two of them holding up?

t_ch: 不太好。毕竟我们亲眼看到了阿格莱雅大人的…最后时刻。
t_en: Not well. After all, we witnessed Lady Aglaea's... last moments.

t_ch: 你看到了，阿格莱雅的…
t_en: You witnessed Aglaea's...

t_ch: 嗯…我们目睹了她最后的时刻。
t_en: Yes... we witnessed her final moments.

t_ch: 我第一时间就前去尝试治疗了，只是连天空祝福都……
t_en: I tried to treat her immediately, but even the sky's blessings...

t_ch: 宣告她的死讯…是我作为医师做过的最艰难的事。
t_en: Declaring her death was the hardest thing I've ever had to do as a doctor.

t_ch: 白厄阁下…别勉强自己。现在你的肩膀更沉重了，千万别倒下……
t_en: Lord Phainon... don't push yourself. You're carrying a heavier burden now. Please don't fall...

t_ch: …没事，我还扛得住。
t_en: ...It's fine. I can handle it.

t_ch: 我失去的是一位导师、战友。而缇宝和缇宁老师，她们失去的是千年的牵绊…如同生命的一部分。
t_en: What I've lost is a mentor, and a comrade-in-arms. But for Tribbie and Trinnon, they've lost a thousand-year-old bond... like losing a part of their lives.

t_ch: 去休息吧，风堇。你应该很长时间没合眼了吧？后面的事，交给我们就好。
t_en: You should rest, Hyacine. You've been awake for a long time, haven't you? We'll handle things from here.

t_ch: 嗯，好。回头见，大家。
t_en: Okay. See you later, everyone.

t_ch: 小白，小灰…你们回来啦。
t_en: Snowy, Little Gray... you're back.

t_ch: 缇宝老师，我……
t_en: Lady Tribbie, I...

t_ch: 小白，不要！请不要和*我们*道歉。
t_en: Snowy, don't! Please don't apologize to us.

t_ch: 你远赴树庭是为了让自己变得强大，强大到能对付威胁到奥赫玛的敌人。你没做错任何事，千万不要苛责自己呀。
t_en: You went to the Grove to prepare yourself for the threats to Okhema. You didn't do anything wrong, so don't be hard on yourself.

t_ch: 这段漫长的旅程，*我们*一直都陪在阿雅身边，比谁都清楚她心中所想。
t_en: We were always by Agy's side throughout this long journey, so we understood her thoughts better than anyone.

t_ch: 她知道自己看不到逐火之旅的尾声，但还是毫无保留地燃烧自己…为了所有人的明天。
t_en: She knew she wouldn't see the end of the Flame-Chase Journey, yet she gave everything without hesitation... for the future of everyone.

t_ch: 我知道阿格莱雅对我的期望。当下，奥赫玛最不需要的就是一个自怨自艾的引路人。
t_en: I know what Aglaea expected of me. Right now, the last thing Okhema needs is a leader wallowing in self-pity.

t_ch: 那边的卷轴，是阿雅托付给*我们*的一则留言。她嘱咐*我们*在特别的时候将它转交给你。
t_en: The scroll over there was entrusted to us by Agy. She instructed us to hand it over to you when the time was right.

t_ch: 我想，现在…就是那个「特别的时候」啦。
t_en: I think... now's the "right time."

t_ch: 阿雅……
t_en: Agy...

t_ch: 那碗燕麦粥的味道…我还记得哦。
t_en: I still remember the taste of that oatmeal...

t_ch: 明早见。到时候，我再帮你准备早餐吧。
t_en: See you tomorrow morning. I'll prepare breakfast for you then.

t_ch: 白厄，我谦逊的学生，信赖的同僚——
t_en: Phainon, my humble student, my trusted colleague —

t_ch: 在我写下这些文字时，你正与元老院的辩论家唇齿交锋，在硝烟无形的战场上争取逐火之旅的存续。
t_en: As I write this, you are locked in a battle of words with the Council of Elders, fighting for the survival of the Flame-Chase Journey on a battlefield where the smoke of war is invisible.

t_ch: 对于将你与{NICKNAME}仓促推上辩论台的决定，我深感惶恐与愧疚。但请你原谅，彼时，我断然不能在你们面前显露出怯懦——因为那定会打击你们的士气。
t_en: I deeply regret and feel guilty for hastily pushing you and {NICKNAME} into the debate. But please forgive me — at that moment, I could not afford to show any sign of weakness before you, as it would have crushed your morale.

t_ch: 我本将这场辩论视为惯常之事，以为仅凭在百年岁月中累积的民意与经验便能取胜。但我既低估了凯妮斯和其党羽的狡黠，也低估了自己通感力的退化。
t_en: I considered the debate a routine affair, assuming I could win merely with the support and experience we'd accumulated over centuries. But I underestimated Caenis and her party's cunning, and also the deterioration of my own senses.

t_ch: 那陷阱大概是辩手卡勒克提斯设下的。他预先准备好了剪断的金线，在凯妮斯辩论陷入下风时突然将它示于众人面前。
t_en: The trap was likely set by the debater Callictis. He had prepared a severed golden thread in advance, revealing it to the public when Caenis was faltering in the debate.

t_ch: 他声称我在用金线阅读众人的思绪，以此在辩论时舞弊。我本有百种方式应对那低级的盘外招，但话语却未经思想的审验便溜出了嘴边……
t_en: He claimed I was reading everyone's thoughts with the golden thread to cheat in the debate. I could have countered that low tactic in a hundred ways, but my words slipped out before I could even think about them...

t_ch: 「正因人群中满是如你这般卑鄙的奸宄，我才需以金线监管这圣城中的一切。」
t_en: "With deceitful scoundrels like you in the crowd, I have no choice but to rely on the golden thread to keep watch over everything in this holy city."

t_ch: 那番话语引起的反响，无需我解释你亦能想象。那一刻，我顿觉自己时日无多。
t_en: You can imagine the reaction that comment provoked. At that moment, I felt my time was running short.

t_ch: 这具躯壳内的神性或许可以永续——但那终究不是「我」的本源。我是人之女，自母亲的胎盘中降生，亦会以人的姿态死去。
t_en: The divinity in this body might be eternal — but that is not the essence of "me." I am a daughter of humanity, born of my mother, and I will die as a human.

t_ch: 意识到自己人性将尽之际，我便开始筹划自己的退场。要以怎样的方式离席，才能不浪费这一场迟来的死亡？我一时还未找到最理想的答案。
t_en: Realizing that my humanity was fading, I began to plan my exit. What kind of departure would not waste such a belated death? I have yet to find the ideal answer.

t_ch: 但我清楚，自己不能与常人一样在睡梦中安然离去。那些毒蛇，它们畏惧被金线割断蛇头，因此才会一直匍匐于黑暗中。
t_en: But I knew I couldn't simply fade away in my sleep like anyone else. The serpents have always lurked in the darkness, terrified of their heads being cut off by the golden thread.

t_ch: 若我的离去平静而无波澜，接踵而至的会是倾巢而出的蛇灾。很遗憾，安享平静的死亡注定是我不可企及的奢侈。
t_en: If my departure were to be calm and uneventful, it would only lead to a swarm of those serpents emerging from their nests. Regrettably, a peaceful death is a luxury I cannot afford.

t_ch: 我必须主动示弱，引得那些毒蛇失去耐心，蠢蠢欲动。当它们以为自己将要得偿所愿，露出毒牙咬向我的脚踵时——我将以最后的火焰点燃蛇巢，焚烧阴影中的威胁。
t_en: I must feign weakness deliberately, to make those serpents lose patience and act rashly. Just as they think they've struck and sunk their fangs into my heels — that's when I'll strike back, igniting the final blaze to burn away the threats hidden in the shadows.

t_ch: 冲天的火光也许会令人们混乱、畏惧，但我并不担心他们会迷失方向。因为奥赫玛还有你在，白厄。
t_en: The blaze soaring into the sky might sow fear and confusion, but I have no worries about people losing their way. After all, Okhema still has you to guide them, Phainon.

t_ch: 你并不完美，白厄。多少个黎明，你的迷茫、鲁莽和多愁善感在我面前上演。沉重的过往压在你的肩上，令你一直痛苦不堪。
t_en: You're not perfect, Phainon. I've witnessed your confusion, recklessness, and deep sensitivity dawn after dawn. The heavy burdens of your past have always weighed you down, inflicting deep pain.

t_ch: 但你也是「完美」的。因为你理解并包容所有人的缺憾，你愿意以不设前提的善意鉴出凡人粗坯之下最为珍贵的品质。
t_en: Yet, in your way, you are perfect. You embrace everyone's imperfections, always ready to find the precious qualities hidden beneath their rough exteriors, unconditionally.

t_ch: 我想，那或许也是天父刻法勒与其它神明有别之处。它发现自己造物身上的残缺，却依旧选择照耀他们，以无条件的博爱守护他们……
t_en: That might be what sets our sky father Kephale apart from other deities. They recognize the imperfections in their creations yet still choose to shine their light on them, offering protection with unconditional love...

t_ch: 白厄，我想让你明白——在你热忱地追逐「纷争」，以为那才是你应当投身的命运时——从始至终，我的想法从未改变：
t_en: Phainon, while you zealously pursued "Strife," believing it to be your destined path — from the very beginning, my thoughts have never changed.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>你是翁法罗斯注定的负世者，通向翁法罗斯未来的门扉。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>You are the destined Worldbearer of Amphoreus, the gate to Amphoreus's future.</color>

t_ch: 去完成神谕中的逐火之旅吧。去点亮星辰，给人子带去希望吧。
t_en: Go complete the Flame-Chase Journey of the prophecy. Ignite the stars and inspire hope in the hearts of humanity.

t_ch: 我会在西风的尽头，盼待由你开辟出的奇迹。
t_en: I will be waiting at the end of the west wind, looking forward to the miracle you will create.

t_ch: 阿格莱雅，我到底何德何能…值得你把炙热的火炬传到我的手上？
t_en: Aglaea, how could I possibly be worthy... of the burning torch you've passed into my hands?

t_ch: 丹恒，能请你帮我一个忙吗？
t_en: Dan Heng, can you do me a favor?

t_ch: 我能帮到什么，尽管提吧。
t_en: Whatever I can help with, just say the word.

t_ch: 请你为克拉特鲁斯阁下带话，让他集结圣城卫士和悬锋族人，挨家挨户地带去消息：明天的明晰时，白厄会在云石天宫宣讲。
t_en: Please deliver a message to Lord Krateros. Have him gather the Holy City Guards and the Kremnoans to spread the word from door to door: At the Lucid Hour tomorrow, Phainon will speak at the Marmoreal Palace.

t_ch: 请他们告诉每个人，凡是对圣城的未来和逐火之旅心存顾虑的公民，都应该来参加这次集会。告诉他们，阿格莱雅将希望的种子留给了翁法罗斯。
t_en: Urge everyone who has concerns about the holy city's future and the Flame-Chase Journey to attend this gathering. Tell them Aglaea has left Amphoreus the seeds of hope.

t_ch: 好，我明白了。
t_en: Understood.

t_ch: 谢谢你。各位都经历了惊魂未定的一天，请务必好好休息，整理一下心情。我还要去做好准……
t_en: Thank you. It's been a harrowing day for everyone. Please make sure to rest well and collect your thoughts. I need to prepare...

t_ch: 白厄——
t_en: Phainon —

t_ch: 哈托努斯来了。
t_en: Chartonus has arrived.

t_ch: …哈托努斯？
t_en: ...Chartonus?

t_ch: 你怎么会来云石天宫？
t_en: What brings you to Marmoreal Palace?

t_ch: 我本想安顿好一切再去找你。有什么急事吗，哈托努斯？
t_en: I planned to get everything settled before coming to see you. Is there something urgent, Chartonus?

t_ch: 想要了解，你的情况。
t_en: How you're holding up, I wanted to see.

t_ch: 比想象中好些，现在看来。
t_en: Better than I expected, you seem.

t_ch: 已经没有余地让我犹豫了。必须行动起来，让所有人知道黄金裔的领导没有崩溃。
t_en: There's no room for doubt anymore. It's time to take action and show everyone that the leadership of the Chrysos Heirs is still strong.

t_ch: 很正确，你的判断。现在，收下它吧。
t_en: Right, your assessment is. Now, here, take this.

t_ch: 这是…你已经完成了？
t_en: Is this... You've completed it?

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>「侵晨」</color>，我为它取的名字。
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>"Dawnmaker"</color> — the name I've chosen for it.

t_ch: 备战，于黎明前。挥剑，为了明天。符合你的身份，黄金裔。
t_en: Before Dawn, prepare for battle. For tomorrow, wield your sword. Befits your stature, it does, Chrysos Heir.
"Dawnmaker"...

t_ch: 不可思议。你明明没有亲眼见过那名黑衣剑客…只是听我用言语复述，竟然就能还原到如此地步吗？
t_en: Unbelievable. You've never seen that black-clad swordmaster... yet you've recreated this so precisely, just from what I described?

t_ch: 精确，你的形容。
t_en: Precise, your description.

t_ch: 为它注入黎明之力，我已经。听从你的意念，它的锋刃将附着金焰。
t_en: Imbued it with the power of Dawn, I already have. Guided by your will, gleam with golden flames, its blade will.

t_ch: 挥舞它，愿你战无不胜。
t_en: Wield it. May triumph always be yours.

t_ch: 谢谢你，哈托努斯——我会用这把长剑守护翁法罗斯的生灵。
t_en: Thank you, Chartonus. I will use this sword to protect the beings of Amphoreus.

t_ch: 另一样东西，还有……
t_en: Another thing, there is...

t_ch: 这又是……
t_en: What's this...

t_ch: 封存于此，阿格莱雅神性的残留。
t_en: Stored here, the remnants of Aglaea's divinity are.

t_ch: 这是她的委托。自己的死亡，她早已预见。
t_en: This was her commission. Her death, she had long foreseen.

t_ch: 她…能听得见我们吗？
t_en: Can she still hear us?

t_ch: 我难以确认。我们知之甚少，有关神性。
t_en: It's difficult to confirm. About divinity, we know very little.

t_ch: 以此种方式，她想陪伴你们，无论如何。
t_en: In this way, she wanted to be with you, always.

t_ch: 我明白了，把它交给我保管吧。这样一来…我身上就又多了一件形影不离之物。
t_en: I understand. Let me keep it. With this... I'll have another constant companion by my side.

t_ch: 很久以前，有人对我说过：「希望这个世界永远都不需要救世主」。
t_en: "May this world never have need for a Deliverer." Someone told me long ago.

t_ch: 但当世界辜负了她的期望、分崩离析的时候，如果看到是我站在人群的前方，带领人们反抗命运……
t_en: But when the world dashed her hopes and fell into disarray, if she could see me now — leading from the front, rallying people against their destiny...

t_ch: …她一定会露出微笑的。
t_en: ...She would surely smile.

t_ch: …看，白厄阁下来了。
t_en: ...Look! Lord Phainon is here.

t_ch: 他会站出来引领奥赫玛么？
t_en: Will he step up to lead Okhema?

t_ch: 没有「金织」女士，圣城该如何延续……
t_en: How will the holy city continue without Lady Goldweaver...

t_ch: 不久以前，我在黎明云崖的公民大会上发表了一番讲话。
t_en: Not long ago, I spoke at the citizens' assembly at Dawncloud.

t_ch: 那时，为了延续黄金裔逐火的希望，我奋力地思考该用什么样的话术博取人们的支持。
t_en: I wrestled with every word, searching for the right ones to rally support and keep the Chrysos Heirs' Flame-Chase alive.

t_ch: 最后，我想起了一位老师的教导：在以口舌为兵器的场合，激愤和盲目是远胜理性的强大武器。
t_en: In the end, I remembered a lesson from one of my teachers: In battles where words are weapons, outrage and blind passion often overpower reason.

t_ch: 所以我采取了他的策略。我将站在对立面的论敌贬低为虫豸，抨击他们的品格，并列数他们的罪行。
t_en: I followed his strategy. I painted my opponents as nothing more than pests, tore apart their character, and laid out their crimes.

t_ch: 我的策略奏效了。它为我争取到了足够多的票数，让我达成了想要的目标。但事后？我没有感到分毫的喜悦。
t_en: The strategy worked. I secured enough votes to achieve my goal. But afterward? There wasn't even a flicker of joy.

t_ch: 因为我很清楚——那场表面上的胜利掩盖了更大的失败。在我为了胜利不顾手段地挑唆对立的情绪时，我已经和自己加入逐火事业的初衷背道而驰。
t_en: I realized — that apparent victory masked a deeper failure. In my pursuit of success, I fueled division and strayed far from my very purpose that led me to the Flame-Chase Journey.

t_ch: 这段征途本应凝聚，而非分裂；它本应令我们强大，而非使我们脆弱。
t_en: This journey was supposed to unify, not divide. To strengthen us, not weaken us.

t_ch: 过去，是金色的丝线在为我们缝补裂痕。有人会将它比作监视的眼线，掌控的工具，但没人能够否认…是阿格莱雅将这座城市，乃至这个支离破碎的世界缝缀在了一起。
t_en: It was the gold thread that mended our rifts in the past. Some saw it as a means of surveillance, a tool of control, but no one can deny that it was Aglaea who held this city, and even this shattered world, together.

t_ch: 但如今她已不在——没错，正如你们听闻的那样——守护圣城千年的半神阿格莱雅，的确已经陨落了。
t_en: Now she is gone — yes, as you have heard — the demigod Aglaea, who guarded the holy city for a millennium, has indeed fallen.

t_ch: 是谁谋害了金织女士？
t_en: Who murdered Lady Goldweaver?

t_ch: 找到凶手！严惩他们！
t_en: Find the murderer! Make them pay!

t_ch: 起初，我的心情也和你们一样。
t_en: I felt the same as all of you initially.

t_ch: 起初，我也想在这里与你们一同痛斥罪人的恶行，商讨该如何追讨他们，在他们的头顶降下惩罚……
t_en: I wanted to stand here beside you, to condemn the sinners, to discuss retribution, and to see justice brought down upon them...

t_ch: …但我的想法改变了。
t_en: ...But my perspective has changed.

t_ch: 此刻，城中的金线已经散去，我却没有看到任何人在为脱离掌控雀跃欢呼。在空荡的市集和浴宫，我只把一种情绪收入眼中：不安。
t_en: The golden threads that once bound this city have unraveled, yet no one is celebrating their freedom from control. In the empty markets and bath chambers, one feeling lingers: Unease.

t_ch: 因为在场的每个人都心知肚明，防止我们被彼此心底阴暗所伤的最后保障已经消失。从此，除了我们为自己捏塑的道德律令，再也没有哪股神力能替我们抵御恶意。
t_en: Deep down, we all know — the last safeguard against the harm we may do to each other is gone. No divine power will protect us from the malice in our hearts. From now on, only the moral code we choose to uphold for ourselves will stand between us and the abyss.

t_ch: 白厄他，好会说呀。
t_en: Phainon has a way with words.

t_ch: 别出声，好好听着。
t_en: Be quiet, and listen carefully.

t_ch: …哦！好吧。
t_en: ...Oh! Alright.

t_ch: 文化人就是不一样。
t_en: There's something special about a man of letters.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，可不是嘛！
t_en: Indeed!

t_ch: 换我上去也能行。
t_en: If it were me up there, I could do it too.

t_ch: …你还真是自信呀？
t_en: ...You think highly of yourself, don't you?

t_ch: 逐火之旅已经接近尾声，黄金裔唯一需要征服的泰坦仅剩{RUBY_B#天空之泰坦}艾格勒{RUBY_E#}一尊。
t_en: The Flame-Chase Journey is nearing its end, with only one Titan left for the Chrysos Heirs to conquer: {RUBY_B#Sky Titan}Aquila{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 可在登临天空以前，我会先向你们发起挑战——我挑战你们心中可能的阴仄与恶意，让你们的心灵先于这片天空放晴！
t_en: Before I rise to the skies, I challenge you — cast out the shadows and malice within your hearts. Let your spirits shine brighter than the sky itself!

t_ch: 请允许我见证：哪怕失去了「浪漫」的神权，奥赫玛仍会是末世中的理想乡。在这里，居住着阿格莱雅所期待的、神谕中的新世界所期待的……
t_en: Let me bear witness: Even without the divine authority of Romance, Okhema will remain a sanctuary in these end times. A place for the kind people Aglaea dreamed of, the kind of people foretold in prophecy...

t_ch: 更好的人！
t_en: A better people!

t_ch: …更好的人！
t_en: ...Better people!

t_ch: 更好的人！更好的人！
t_en: Better people! Better people!

t_ch: 更好的人！更好的人！更好的人！
t_en: Better people! Better people! Better people!

t_ch: 你们愿意接受挑战…我心存感激。你们令我醒悟：阿格莱雅留给我的或许是世上最质朴的职责。
t_en: I appreciate your willingness to accept the challenge... You have reminded me that Aglaea left me perhaps the simplest duty in the world.

t_ch: 要背负创世的命运，我只需付出一样东西。那是她曾慷慨地赠予我的礼物，而我也会转交给你们每个人：<color=#dbc291ff>毫无保留的深信。</color>
t_en: To bear the burden of creation, I need only to give one thing. It's a gift she once gave me without hesitation, and now I share it with each of you — <color=#dbc291ff>unshakable faith.</color>

t_ch: 距离翁法罗斯的新生还有一段长路。但我们不会在前进时遗忘过去，因为翁法罗斯的记忆总能造福后人。
t_en: There's still a long way to go before Amphoreus is reborn. But we will not forget the past as we move forward, for the memories of Amphoreus will always benefit future generations.

t_ch: 请将这一刻借给我吧。我想征用各位几分钟的光阴，一起缅怀一位庇佑圣城千年的半神……
t_en: Allow me this moment. I want to borrow a few minutes of your time to commemorate a demigod who protected the holy city for a millennium...

t_ch: …她是奥赫玛的改衣师，黄金裔的领袖，「金织」阿格莱雅。而她这一生最大的壮举——
t_en: ...She was Okhema's dressmaker, the leader of the Chrysos Heirs, the Goldweaver Aglaea. And her greatest feat —

t_ch: ——是成为一个无私的人。
t_en: — was being a selfless person.

t_ch: 这歌声…好熟悉呀！
t_en: That singing sounds so familiar!

t_ch: 我们的歌谣，小白以前最喜欢啦！
t_en: That's our song, and Snowy used to love it!

t_ch: 是啊，缇安老师。对于那个心里充满不安的孩子……
t_en: Yes, Lady Trianne. For that child filled with anxiety...

t_ch: …你们的歌谣总能驱散我心里的阴云，哪怕只有短短的一刻。
t_en: ...your songs always dispelled the clouds in my heart, even if just for a moment.

t_ch: 嘿嘿…小时候的小白，很可爱，很善良！
t_en: Hehe... Snowy was so cute and kind when he was little!

t_ch: 现在…唔，变得好大一只，没那么可爱了？但还是很善良哇！
t_en: Now... hmm, you've grown so much. Maybe not as cute but still just as kind!

t_ch: 哈哈哈…岁月催人老，这谁都没办法。但善意…是你们帮我滋养了它。
t_en: Hahaha... Time spares no one, so there's nothing we can do. But kindness... you helped nurture it in me.

t_ch: 不用谢啦！你的光芒，很耀眼！继续，保持！
t_en: Don't mention it! Your light is as bright as ever. Keep it up and stay strong!

t_ch: 再见啦，小白~
t_en: See you, Snowy.

t_ch: 再见，缇安老师。
t_en: Goodbye, Lady Trianne.

t_ch: 听你刚才那番话，是觉得我教给你的辩论术是邪门歪道么？
t_en: I heard what you said. Are you suggesting the debate tactics I've taught you are nothing but sleight of hand?

t_ch: 我可没那么说啊，那刻夏老师。毕竟要是没有你传授给我的技巧，逐火之旅还存不存在都两说了。
t_en: Not at all, Professor Anaxa. Honestly, without those techniques you taught me, the Flame-Chase Journey might not have survived.

t_ch: 哼…还好你脑子足够清晰。
t_en: Hmph... Good thing your head's still clear.

t_ch: 对应刻法勒的黄金裔么…呵，当初在教室里按着你的脑袋背公式的时候，可真没想到你还是这块料。
t_en: The Chrysos Heir of Kephale... I had no idea you had it in you while I was drilling those formulas in your head in class.

t_ch: 谁能想得到呢，老师？顺带一说，那些公式我现在也已经忘得一干二净了。
t_en: Who could have thought, Professor? By the way, I've completely forgotten those formulas now.

t_ch: 呵，真是不可教也。来世，让我好好教你该如何承担神职吧。
t_en: Ha, some things never change. In our next lives, I'll teach you how to truly embrace your divine duties.

t_ch: 哈哈哈…那我等着。
t_en: Hahaha... I'll be waiting.

t_ch: 再见，那刻夏老师。
t_en: Goodbye, Professor Anaxa.

t_ch: …遐蝶小姐？
t_en: ...Miss Castorice?

t_ch: 你…不说些什么吗？
t_en: Aren't you going to say something?

t_ch: 白厄阁下，你…总是那么平易近人。
t_en: Lord Phainon, you... have always been so approachable.

t_ch: 自我们认识起，你就一直将我视作伙伴…我在你身上第一次体会到了什么是普通人间的友谊。
t_en: Ever since we first met, you've seen me as a friend... It was the first time I understood what everyday friendship was like.

t_ch: 但那…也很危险。
t_en: But that's... dangerous.

t_ch: 危险？
t_en: Dangerous?

t_ch: 因为和你一起说笑时，我偶尔会忘记自己是谁…对于拥有我这般力量的人，那非常危险。
t_en: Laughing and chatting with you, I sometimes forget who I am... and for someone with my kind of power, that's a real hazard.

t_ch: 啊…原来还有这一层意思。抱歉，我忘记换位思考了。
t_en: Ah... I hadn't thought of it that way. Sorry, I forgot to consider your perspective.

t_ch: 请别道歉…因为尽管如此，那些我们一起度过的时光…回想起来还是十分令我开心。
t_en: Please, no apologies... Despite everything, thinking back on the times we spent together makes me really happy.

t_ch: …我又何尝不是呢。
t_en: ...I feel the same.

t_ch: 再见了，白厄。
t_en: Goodbye, Phainon.

t_ch: 再见，遐蝶。
t_en: Goodbye, Castorice.

t_ch: 哼…忘了谁了？
t_en: Hmph... Did you forget me?

t_ch: 哈，忘了你？那怎么可能。
t_en: Ha, forget you? Like I'd ever.

t_ch: 咱们之间又不是生离死别，你只是去悬锋城看门了而已，就别掺和这么催人泪下的场合了。
t_en: We aren't parting ways forever. You're just off to guard the gates at Castrum Kremnos. Let's not make this a tearful farewell.

t_ch: 呵…你还是那么懂得如何令人不快啊。
t_en: Heh... you know just how to make a person uncomfortable.

t_ch: 我有预感，你我在此世还会再见。有机会时再较量一番吧，「救世主」。
t_en: I have a feeling we'll meet again in this lifetime. Let's have another showdown then, "Deliverer."

t_ch: 这么自信？现在的我可不比以往了。看见这把剑了吗？这可是刚出哈托努斯锻炉的极品。
t_en: That's very confident of you. I've changed since then. Have you seen this sword? It's a masterpiece, freshly forged by Chartonus.

t_ch: 哈…尚未夺得泰坦神权的区区凡夫，口气倒是狂妄得很。
t_en: Ha... A bold statement for a mere mortal who hasn't yet claimed the power of a Titan.

t_ch: 回头见，哀丽秘榭的白厄。
t_en: See you around, Phainon of Aedes Elysiae.

t_ch: 约好了，迈德漠斯。
t_en: It's a date, Mydeimos.
Hehe...

t_ch: …阿格莱雅。
t_en: ...Aglaea.

t_ch: 要记得，白厄…完成逐火以后，要把开启创世奇迹的决定交由翁法罗斯的人们。
t_en: Remember, Phainon... after completing the Flame-Chase, leave the decision to initiate the Miracle of Genesis to the Amphoreans.

t_ch: 黄金裔是神谕的代行者，而他们——他们才是这个世界的主人。
t_en: The Chrysos Heirs are the executors of the prophecy, yet they — the people are the true masters of this world.

t_ch: 放心吧。我一直记得你的教诲——
t_en: Rest assured. I always keep your teachings in mind.

t_ch: 「自命不凡的英雄，与桀恶仅有一线之隔」，对么？
t_en: "The difference between a presumptuous hero and a tyrant is a fine line." Right?

t_ch: 你记得很清楚…这很好。
t_en: Well said. You've got a good memory.

t_ch: 已经这个时候了…你没有什么别的话想说吗？
t_en: It's late... Anything else you'd like to add?

t_ch: 别的话么……
t_en: Anything else...?

t_ch: 哪怕只是在你的想象中…我也不擅表露自己脆弱的一面啊。
t_en: Even in your imagination... I'm not one to show my vulnerable side.

t_ch: 哈…没关系，让你说出一句毫无遮掩的交心话到底有多难，这我早就知道了。
t_en: Ha... no worries. I've long understood how difficult it is for you to open up completely.

t_ch: 谢谢你把我带到了这里，阿格莱雅…你拯救了我。
t_en: Thank you for bringing me here, Aglaea... You saved me.

t_ch: 呵…也许我想要拯救的不是你，孩子……
t_en: Heh... maybe what I wanted to save wasn't you, child...

t_ch: …而是这个摇摇欲坠的世界。
t_en: ...but this crumbling world.

t_ch: 再见，白厄。
t_en: Goodbye, Phainon.

t_ch: 再见，阿格莱雅。
t_en: Goodbye, Aglaea.

t_ch: …真是一段感伤，却又精彩的旅途呀。
t_en: ...What a poignant, yet splendid journey.

t_ch: 而且还没结束…对吗？
t_en: And it's still not over yet... is it?

t_ch: 对，昔涟。
t_en: Yes, Cyrene.

t_ch: 果然还是被我说中了吧？最后的最后，你还是成为了这个故事里的救世主呀。
t_en: I was right. In the end, you did become the Deliverer in this story.

t_ch: 你不在的时间里…很多事都变了。我认识了各种各样的人，也做了很多不同的事。我拾起了一些决心…也放下了一些执念。
t_en: A lot has changed while you were away. I've met so many people and tried so many things. I've found new resolve... and I've let go of some attachments.

t_ch: 我改变了很多。
t_en: I'm not the same person I used to be.
Hee...

t_ch: 但是，在我看来——
t_en: But to me —

t_ch: 你从来都没有变哦，白厄。
t_en: You've never changed at all, Phainon.

t_ch: 几日后，黎明云崖……
t_en: Days later, at Dawncloud...

t_ch: 呵，黎明云崖…我对这里的记忆还真是百味交杂。
t_en: Ah, Dawncloud... This place holds so many different kinds of memories.

t_ch: 还记得，那个时候…从这里往山下望，一眼就能辨认出「金织」的招牌。
t_en: I remember you could see the "Goldweaver" sign from here, all the way down the mountain.

t_ch: 一千年过去，我的眼神也不如从前那么锐利了哪……
t_en: But my eyes aren't what they used to be a thousand years ago...

t_ch: 赛法利娅，你……
t_en: Cifera, you...

t_ch: 裁缝女，我问你！
t_en: Seamstress! Tell me!

t_ch: 奥赫玛有没有什么稀罕的宝贝，是连你…唔…连我们的店里都没有的？
t_en: Does Okhema have any treasures so rare that not even your... I mean, our shop, has seen?

t_ch: 当然。我听闻，在黎明云崖的某处藏着一颗「全世宝石」，传说它是从黎明机器上脱落的碎屑。
t_en: Of course, it has. I heard there's a "Gem of All Worlds" hidden somewhere in Dawncloud. Apparently it's a piece that fell out of the Dawn Device.

t_ch: 作为刻法勒神躯的一部分，它是真正的无价之宝，即便「金织」也无从染指它的珠光。
t_en: It's truly a priceless treasure, being a part of Kephale's divine body. Even I, the Goldweaver, cannot witness its glamour.

t_ch: 「全世宝石」……
t_en: "Gem of All Worlds"...

t_ch: …哈，我记下啦！那你接着忙店里的事，我先……
t_en: ...Nice! Got it! Well, please keep looking after the shop while I—

t_ch: …让我猜猜你在想什么吧，赛法利娅。
t_en: ...Let me guess, Cifera.

t_ch: 你大概已经在盘算要如何混入黎明云崖，将那颗宝石收入囊中了，对么？
t_en: You're probably already plotting to sneak into Dawncloud to steal that gem, aren't you?
...Erm.

t_ch: 你大概还想到了下一步：要把它卖出一个好价钱，然后把得来的财富分给圣城的穷人、难民……
t_en: And your next move is probably trying to sell it to the highest bidder, then distribute that money to the poor and the refugees in the holy city.

t_ch: …裁缝女，你真的没在用金线偷看我的想法嘛？
t_en: ...Look, seamstress. Are you sure you aren't peeking into my head with your golden threads?

t_ch: 你擅长的只是掩饰谎言。可你的同情心，一直跃然脸上。
t_en: You might be skilled at covering up lies, but your compassion is all over your face, plain as day.

t_ch: 不要蒙蔽自己。打你的心底…你认为这样做是正确的吗，赛法利娅？
t_en: Don't lie to yourself. Do you honestly think this is the right thing to do, Cifera?

t_ch: …那我还能怎么办？以我的力量能做到的…就只有这些而已。
t_en: ...What else am I supposed to do? That's the only thing I can do with my power!

t_ch: 裁缝女，你常说，我有成为一个好人，甚至是成为大家眼中的英雄的潜质……
t_en: You keep talking about how I have the potential to become a good person, or even what people call a "hero"...

t_ch: 但我不知道该怎么做…我没法像你那样织出金光闪闪的布匹，也没有海瑟音姐姐那么美妙的歌喉，更不可能像缇宝阿姐那样知识渊博……
t_en: But I don't know what I need to do to get there... I can't weave golden dresses like you, I can't sing as well as Miss Hysilens, and I definitely don't know as much as big sis Tribbie...

t_ch: 我唯一擅长的东西…就只有在街头领悟的，那些在大家眼里见不得光的活计而已！
t_en: The only things I'm good at are the underhanded street smarts that no one even respects!

t_ch: 可即便如此，我也想…想配得上你的选择。小偷也有做梦的权利，对吧……
t_en: But... but I still want to be worthy of you. I want to prove that you've made the right choice. Even thieves can dream, right...?
Tsk...

t_ch: 我明白，赛法利娅…我明白。
t_en: I know, Cifera... I know.

t_ch: 你有千万个理由控诉，因为宿命就是如此不公。你要走的道路注定更为曲折…在这趟旅程中，你势必要在背光处承受人们的误解。
t_en: You are justified in your anger. Destiny is never fair, and your path is doomed to be even worse than everyone else's. You are fated to walk in the dark and bear the world's misunderstanding in this journey.

t_ch: 但你要相信一件事：每个人都是世上独一无二的存在。
t_en: But you have to believe that everyone in this world is unique in their own way.

t_ch: 即便算上多洛斯传说中的三百侠盗…我依然坚信，你会是唯一能够承载「诡计」神职的命运之女。
t_en: I am convinced that you're the only one who can bear the authority of Trickery, even if you have to contend with the legendary three hundred master thieves of Dolos.

t_ch: 阿…阿格莱雅……
t_en: A–Aglaea...

t_ch: 要是能让所有人都相信我就好了…我想让大家都认为，世界是个美好的地方…世上的人们都很单纯善良。
t_en: I just want everyone to believe that the world is beautiful and that everyone is simple and kind... If only I could get everyone to believe that...

t_ch: 哪怕我知道那是假话，但只要相信的人足够多…谎言是不是就能成真…？
t_en: Would that lie become reality if enough people believed in it? ...Even though it's a lie?

t_ch: 谎言永远都是谎言，赛法利娅……
t_en: Lies will always be lies, Cifera.

t_ch: 只是，在某些时候…它的确可以比坦诚高贵。
t_en: But sometimes... they can be more noble than the truth.

t_ch: …你打算就一直在那儿站着？恕我直言，鬼鬼祟祟的风格可不适合你——救世小子。
t_en: ...Are you really planning to just stand there forever? Honestly, sneaking around doesn't suit you, Deliverer.

t_ch: 哈哈哈…我可没想瞒过你的耳朵，赛飞儿小姐。我只是隐隐感觉到你被思绪缠住了，不想贸然打扰。
t_en: Ahaha... I never thought I could slip past your sharp ears, Miss Cipher. I just didn't think it'd be right to interrupt when you're obviously lost in thoughts.

t_ch: 哈…小子，说话还是这么招人待见哪。你之前在大浴场的宣讲，还不赖。我承认，自己有那么一丢丢被触动到。
t_en: Hmph. Still as charming with your words as ever, boy. Your speech at the public baths wasn't half bad, I'll admit. It managed to touch my heart just a smidge.

t_ch: 多谢夸奖。直觉是样神奇的东西，当时我就隐约感觉到，你也在场。
t_en: Thank you. I had a feeling back then that you were in the audience. Intuition certainly is a thing of wonder.

t_ch: …哦？那你怎么没当时就把我叫出来，居然还等到现在？
t_en: ...Oh? And you didn't call me out on the spot? You waited all this time to tell me?

t_ch: 因为…我猜你也需要一段时间来整理好心情，悼念她的离开。
t_en: Well... I thought you'd need some time to yourself... after she passed away.

t_ch: 哼，多管闲事。
t_en: Hm. None of your business.

t_ch: 寒暄到此为止。说吧，你遇上什么麻烦了，需要我这个大前辈帮忙？
t_en: Enough small talk. Tell me, what kind of trouble did you run into that you need me, the super-old-timer, to step in?

t_ch: 你我一共就见过几次面，每次都是直奔主题…我欣赏你的直爽，「前辈」——那我就有话直说了。
t_en: We've only met a couple of times, but you've always gone straight to business. I respect that about you, "old-timer," so I'll cut to the chase.

t_ch: 如你所见，阿格莱雅为黄金裔挣得了全城公民的支持。人们一致认为是元老院中反对逐火的派别谋害了她，公民的激愤让藏在阴影中的险恶无处可躲。
t_en: You're probably aware that Aglaea has earned the support of the entire city for the Chrysos Heirs. Everyone believes that she died at the hands of Council members who are against the Flame-Chase, and the citizens' fury has been dragging these schemers out of the shadows one by one.

t_ch: 可即便如此，警报也没有彻底解除。「清洗者」的领袖，元老凯妮斯——我们至今没能找到她的下落。
t_en: But they didn't get everybody. To this day, we still haven't found Elder Caenis — the leader of the "Cleaners."

t_ch: 噢，我明白了。你怕她会狗急跳墙，对吧？
t_en: Ah. I see. You're afraid she'll do something wild now that she's pushed into a corner, aren't you?

t_ch: 是。刻法勒的火种一直被供奉在议会剧场的上空，我想它会是反对者们阻挠黄金裔的最后底牌。
t_en: Yes. I believe that Kephale's Coreflame, forever enshrined above the council theater, would be the opposition's final trump card against the Chrysos Heirs.

t_ch: 再过几日，我们就要启程征讨{RUBY_B#天空之泰坦}艾格勒{RUBY_E#}了。黄金裔远离圣城的这段时间，是他们下手的最佳时机……
t_en: We're setting out to campaign against {RUBY_B#Sky Titan}Aquila{RUBY_E#} in a few days. With the Chrysos Heirs gone, it'll be their best chance to strike...

t_ch: 你害怕那些落魄的野狗会趁你们离开的时候偷走火种——
t_en: You think those desperate dogs will try to snatch the Coreflame while you're gone —

t_ch: ——所以你想找我帮忙，在你缺席的时间里遛遛他们，对么？
t_en: — So you want me to give them a nice long walk while you're away, right?

t_ch: 每一点都被押中了，不愧是你。我清楚你的力量，赛飞儿小姐：神速、伪装、来去无形…还有那最让人捉摸不透的，「欺骗」的权柄。
t_en: You sure saw right through me, Miss Cipher. I know exactly what you can do. God-like speed, perfect disguises, untraceable movements... And that most enigmatic authority of "Trickery."

t_ch: 请尽管动用你的手段，哪怕要把我们也连带着一起蒙蔽。我只有一个请求：请保护好刻法勒的火种。
t_en: I have only one request. Please, use whatever tricks you need to protect Kephale's Coreflame, even if you have to deceive the rest of the Chrysos Heirs as well.

t_ch: 嗯哼……
t_en: Uh-huh...

t_ch: 我说，救世小子，你是不是打心底觉得…我压根没有拒绝你的可能？
t_en: Hey, Deliverer. Do you honestly think that there's no way I'll turn you down?

t_ch: 要是我不打算管这摊子麻烦事，你还有什么后备计划？
t_en: Do you even have any Plan Bs if I decide to nope out on this?

t_ch: 身为「诡计」的半神，恐怕这世界上没有比你更具威力的底牌了——你认为呢，赛飞儿小姐？
t_en: As the demigod of Trickery, there are no other trump cards in the world that can out-rank you — I'm sure you know the answer to your own question, Miss Cipher.

t_ch: 一副掌控全局的自信啊…你算是学到了半点她的精髓。
t_en: What confidence. You talk as if you have everything in the palm of your hand... Hmph, so you did learn a thing or two from her. I'll give you that.

t_ch: 那就来说些你不知道的事吧。「盗火行者」——据我观察，那家伙可是对奥赫玛虎视眈眈哪。
t_en: Then let me tell you something you don't know. The Flame Reaver — they've been watching Okhema closely.

t_ch: 你的意思是……
t_en: You mean...

t_ch: 猜不到吧？我一直盯着那家伙的行踪呢。而我得出的结论……
t_en: Bet you didn't expect that, huh? I've been tracking them all this time, and this is my conclusion.

t_ch: 那家伙是像条疯狗一样丧失理智，但它绝对不是傻子。你猜它为什么一直没对奥赫玛出手？
t_en: That lunatic may have lost their mind, but they're no fool. Why do you think they haven't made a move on Okhema yet?

t_ch: 那刻夏老师分析过这事。当时的结论…大概是因为刻法勒的火种有元老院的秘法保护？
t_en: Professor Anaxa once analyzed this. He thought... It was probably because Kephale's Coreflame was protected by the Council of Elders' secret arts?

t_ch: 哈！什么糠包秘法，我动动手指就能破了它的机关。
t_en: Ha! Secret arts my foot. I could break those mechanisms with a flick of my finger.

t_ch: 告诉你吧，那家伙之所以没有贸然进攻奥赫玛——多半是因为裁缝女织成的防御网比你们想象中的还要坚固。
t_en: Allow me to let you in on the actual secret. They probably held off from attacking Okhema because the seamstress's defense network was way tougher than you thought.

t_ch: 如今她不在了，而你们又打算在城防漏洞百出时出征「晨昏之眼」…啧啧。
t_en: Now that she's gone... and you're heading out to battle the Eye of Twilight... with the city's defenses in shambles... tsk tsk tsk.

t_ch: …真是进退两难的处境啊。
t_en: ...What a dangerous situation we find ourselves in.

t_ch: 但既然你主动抛出了话头…我猜，你一定已经想到了应对之法？
t_en: But since you're the one who brought it up, I'm guessing that you already have a solution in mind.

t_ch: 你现在还真是冷静得不像样哪……
t_en: Just look how ridiculously calm you are right now...

t_ch: 但你没猜错——就把这当做买一赠一的特别服务吧。除了保护刻法勒的火种…我会再卖你们一个人情，顺带陪那条疯狗玩玩。
t_en: But you guessed it right. Consider this a two-for-one deal. I'll keep that lunatic entertained on top of protecting Kephale's Coreflame — You owe a big favor after this.

t_ch: 感激不尽。那盗火者实力莫测，请务必小心。
t_en: You have my deepest gratitude. That Flame Reaver's power is truly unfathomable. Please be careful.

t_ch: 记住咯，救世小子，这个世上有一条真理：「赛法利娅总会笑到最后」。没必要为一位半神操心，我会完成我该做的事，所以——
t_en: Don't forget, Deliverer. There's a fundamental truth in this world. "Cifera will always get the last laugh." No need to worry for a demigod. I'll do my job, so you —

t_ch: 在支付等价的报酬以前，你们可别先死了哪。
t_en: Need to make sure you don't die before you pay me.

t_ch: 丹恒，他先我们一步回来了。
t_en: Dan Heng came back before us.

t_ch: 也许他打听到了什么……
t_en: Maybe he's heard something...

t_ch: 浴场，空荡荡的……
t_en: The baths are empty...

t_ch: 听闻如此噩耗，也没有公民有心情入浴了。
t_en: No citizen feels like bathing after hearing such terrible news.

t_ch: 接下来…该去找缇宝老师了。
t_en: Next... we should visit Lady Tribbie.

t_ch: 怎么铺子都空了？
t_en: Why's the market look so emptied out?

t_ch: 这种时候，哪还有心思开店啊。
t_en: As if anyone's keeping shop nowadays.

t_ch: 外出远征辛苦了…
t_en: Rough expedition, eh?

t_ch: 该不会是他们起了内讧…
t_en: Could it be that they're infighting...

t_ch: 小声点，人都回来了…
t_en: Hush, they're back...

t_ch: 圣城…不应该很安全吗？
t_en: Shouldn't the holy city be safe?

t_ch: 危险的不是黑潮，是人啊…
t_en: The black tide's not the threat... it's the people...

t_ch: 英雄大人们…还请节哀。
t_en: Heroes... our hearts bleed with yours.

t_ch: 凶手？除了元老院还能是谁？
t_en: The murderer? The Council of Elders, obviously.

t_ch: 太歹毒了，那帮权力的走狗…
t_en: Evil monsters. Power-crazed lapdogs...

t_ch: 三位英雄，你们回来了。
t_en: Heroes, you're back.

t_ch: 风堇大人一直守在上面…
t_en: Lady Hyacine's been staying above...

t_ch: 阿雅……
t_en: Agy...

t_ch: 你真的…不用再吃药了吗？
t_en: So, you... really don't have to take the medicine anymore?

t_ch: 不必了，吾师。药物终究只能稳固我的血肉，但阻止不了灵魂的衰竭。
t_en: No need, teacher. Medicine can only steady my flesh, but it cannot stop the decay of my soul.

t_ch: 我已向这世界续借了太多时光——现在，大限到了。
t_en: I've already borrowed too much time from this world... And now, the time is up.

t_ch: 此刻…是我最好的离席时机。
t_en: Now... is the best time for my exit.

t_ch: 呜…呜呜……
t_en: *sob*... *sniffle*...

t_ch: 请别哭泣，吾师……
t_en: Please don't cry on my account, teacher...

t_ch: 我还有…最后一个任性的请求。可以听我诉说吗？
t_en: I have... a final selfish request. Can you lend me an ear?

t_ch: 呜…阿雅……
t_en: *sniffle* Agy...

t_ch: 你说吧…呜呜…*我们*一定会…帮你实现的。
t_en: Go ahead... *sob* We will... definitely fulfill it for you.

t_ch: 我希望自己的死亡平静而轻柔。我希望自己了无生机的死躯…不要曝光于众人的视线之下。
t_en: I wish for my death to be quiet and gentle. I hope that my lifeless body... will not be exposed to the eyes of the public.

t_ch: 吾师，我的一生中经历了太多的改变。但惟有对一件事的追求，我千年如一……
t_en: Teacher, I have faced too many changes in my lifetime. But in the pursuit of one thing alone, I have remained unchanged for a thousand years...

t_ch: …「美丽」，对吗？
t_en: ...It's "beauty," isn't it?
Huh...

t_ch: 没错，吾师…「美丽」。
t_en: That's right, teacher. "Beauty."

t_ch: 阿雅，你觉得小白他…准备好了吗？
t_en: Agy, do you think Snowy is... ready?

t_ch: 千年前，我接过墨涅塔火种的时候…你认为我那时准备好了吗，吾师？
t_en: A thousand years ago, when I took up the Coreflame of Mnestia... do you think I was ready then, teacher?

t_ch: 生在这个时代，我们不得不于骇浪中成长，而非做好一切准备后再启航。
t_en: Born into this era, we had no choice but to grow amidst the raging waves, not set sail after everything was prepared.

t_ch: 白厄，他会明白的。他会理解我留给他的职责…他已足够成熟，不会再被愤怒或仇恨蒙蔽双眼。
t_en: Phainon — he will understand. He will grasp the duty I've left to him... He is mature enough now, no longer blinded by anger or hatred.

t_ch: 与此同时，在神悟树庭……
t_en: Meanwhile, in the Grove of Epiphany...

t_ch: 我回来了……
t_en: I'm back...

t_ch: 自从树庭遭到黑潮破坏，我就一直想尽量远离这里，远离可能让人感伤的回忆。
t_en: I've been trying to stay away from here since the black tide destroyed the Grove... And through that, stay away from the memories that might make me sad.

t_ch: 可风儿却接连把我吹了回来…大概这就是昏光庭院的使命吧？
t_en: But the wind keeps pulling me back... Maybe that's the Twilight Courtyard's duty, after all.

t_ch: 嘟…嘟嘟。
t_en: Doot... Doot-Doot.

t_ch: 我明白，小伊卡——我也很怀念以前和大家一起上课的日子。
t_en: I know, Little Ica. I know... I too miss the days when we all studied together.

t_ch: 不过我们这次前来，可是还有更重要的任务哦。白厄阁下已经选定了征伐{RUBY_B#天空之泰坦}艾格勒{RUBY_E#}的时日，我作为天空一族的后人……
t_en: But we have an even more important mission this time round. Lord Phainon has already set the date to campaign against {RUBY_B#Sky Titan}Aquila{RUBY_E#}. Being a descendant of the Sky people, I...

t_ch: 嘟嘟！嘟！
t_en: Doot-Doot! Doot!
Hmm...

t_ch: 你说得对，稍微走一会儿神的时间总还是有的。
t_en: You're right. At least I can still afford to get lost in thought once in a while.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>先祖们，请稍等我片刻吧</color>。我在树庭留下的回忆…值得花上一些时间来缅怀。
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>O ancestors, please wait for just a bit longer</color>. The time I spent in the Grove... deserves some reminiscence.

t_ch: 嘟…嘟嘟！
t_en: Doot... Doot-Doot!

t_ch: 啊，我知道啦。聚焦眼下，不乱跑。
t_en: Oh. Got it. Yes, I'll focus and not run off.

t_ch: 嘟嘟…嘟？
t_en: Doot-Doot... Doot?

t_ch: 我还记得，就是在这里，那刻夏老师给我们上了「那一课」。
t_en: This was where Professor Anaxa gave us "the lesson."

t_ch: 当时的情境…还历历在目呢。
t_en: I can still see that day in my mind...

t_ch: …以上，就是你们在树庭的最后一堂课了。
t_en: ...And that was your last lesson in the Grove.

t_ch: 从今往后，你们都有各自的路要走。从这里学去的知识能帮上多少忙，全看几位参悟的本领。
t_en: Now, each of you will head off on your own path. How much the Grove's teachings can help you in the real world depends on how well you can put knowledge into practice.

t_ch: 不过，我告诫你们——假如未来你们做出了什么失格之举，千万别对外宣扬你们是七贤人之一，阿那克萨戈拉斯的学生……
t_en: However, heed this warning — If you ever do anything disgraceful out there, don't ever claim you were the student of Anaxagoras, one of the Seven Sages-

t_ch: …那刻夏老师，我看日程表上明明还有其它课啊？你是不是搞错了什……
t_en: ...Professor Anaxa, there are still other classes scheduled on the calendar! Are you sure you didn't —

t_ch: ——首先，叫我阿那克萨戈拉斯教授。
t_en: —Firstly, call me Professor Anaxagoras.

t_ch: 其次，更正一下：最后一课代表的是「由我执教的最后一堂课」。从今天起，我要开始闭关研习古代炼金术，一切教学活动都要为之让路。
t_en: Secondly, to clarify — The "last lesson" means "the last lesson I will teach you." I will be entering seclusion from today onwards to research ancient alchemy, and all my teaching activities will be put on hold.

t_ch: 啊，我懂了。教授的言下之意，是在暗讽其他老师的课程都够不上格吧？
t_en: Ah, I see. So you're saying the lessons taught by all the other teachers aren't good enough to be considered lessons?

t_ch: 尽喜欢耍小聪明和嘴皮功夫——哀丽秘榭的白厄，扣一个学分！
t_en: All you do every day is wag that sharp tongue of yours — That's a credit deduction, Phainon of Aedes Elysiae!
Hee...

t_ch: 言止于此。那么，下……
t_en: I have nothing left to say. Next-

t_ch: …请等一下，教授！
t_en: ...Please wait, Professor!

t_ch: 根据树庭的讲义规范，授课教师在最后一个课时的结尾，应当「与学生畅谈卒业后之理想，引导学生走上妥实之道路。」
t_en: According to the Grove's protocols for a cohort's final class, the lecturer should take some time in that lesson to "discuss the students' post-graduation aspirations with them and guide them toward a solid path."

t_ch: 这么重要的环节可不能漏了呀，教授？
t_en: Surely you can't skip such a crucial step, Professor?
Tsk...

t_ch: 作为助讲的风堇小姐…真是那刻夏老师的克星呀。
t_en: The teaching assistant Miss Hyacine is truly Professor Anaxa's nemesis.

t_ch: …咳，好吧。既然雅辛忒丝如此要求…那就来走一遍流程吧。
t_en: ...Ahem, fine. Since Hyacinthia insists on this... Let's go through the process.

t_ch: 我看某些人的表达欲如此旺盛…不妨由你先来，哀丽秘榭的白厄——提问：你的理想是什么？
t_en: Since some of you are so eager to speak, why don't we start with you, Phainon of Aedes Elysiae? What is your post-graduation aspiration?

t_ch: 我？
t_en: Me?

t_ch: 嗯…理想倒算不上。我唯一想做的，就是保护好身边所有关心的……
t_en: Well... it's not exactly an aspiration, but the only thing I want to do is to protect the people I care about...

t_ch: …明白了，你想当「英雄」。无聊至极的答案，下一个！
t_en: ...I see. So you want to be a "hero." How boring. Next!

t_ch: 呃，我可没说……
t_en: Uh, I didn't say...

t_ch: 呵呵……
t_en: Heehee...

t_ch: 遐蝶！你从刚才起就一直在偷笑，不如接下来就由你来分享吧？
t_en: Castorice! You've been giggling for quite some time. How about you go next?

t_ch: 也许我的回答有些空泛，没有意义，阿那克萨戈拉斯教授……
t_en: Perhaps my answer is a bit vague and meaningless, Professor Anaxagoras, but...

t_ch: 我希望这世界能摆脱「死亡」的痛苦，每一位生者都能拥抱甜美的宁静……
t_en: I hope the world can be freed from the agony of "death," so that every living being may find calming peace...

t_ch: 天真…天真到让人想为你的童话故事掉泪。
t_en: How naive. So naive that it makes me want to weep for your fairy tale.

t_ch: 看吧！这就是我不想参考那册陈腐讲义的原因，因为我得到的尽是些无趣的答案。
t_en: See? This is why I don't want to follow those outdated protocols. All I get are boring answers.

t_ch: 雅辛忒丝——既然建议由你提出，那不妨也以你作收尾——将你的「理想」与在座诸位分享吧，让我们看看你的答案有没有过人之处。
t_en: Since you suggested this whole process, why don't you wrap this up, Hyacinthia? Share your "aspiration" with everyone, and let's see if yours stands out.

t_ch: 最后，果然轮到我了吗……
t_en: So it's finally my turn...

t_ch: 呵呵，幸好我早有准备，可不会被难倒——我的理想，是补全英雄史诗最后的「空白页」。
t_en: Good thing I came prepared, hehe! My aspiration is nothing less than completing the final "blank page" at the end of the heroes' epic.

t_ch: …「空白页」？
t_en: ..."Blank page"?

t_ch: 这说法倒是有趣。不妨再多解释一下吧，助讲。
t_en: An interesting description. Do explain, assistant.

t_ch: 在树庭求学的这段时间里，我读遍翁法罗斯的英雄史诗，也发现了它们的千篇一律之处——
t_en: I've read all of Amphoreus' heroic epics while studying at the Grove, and a common thread came to my attention.

t_ch: 几乎每段史诗的结尾都以英雄的功业作结，却极少有人提到，在他们开创的时代里，芸芸众生如何度日，那些平凡的人们又走过了怎样的艰辛或苦难。
t_en: Almost every epic ends with a description of the hero's achievements, but few mention how the everyday people of that time lived. No one recorded the struggles and suffering the commoners endured.

t_ch: 你是想说，比起名垂青史的英雄，你更关注历史边角的普通人？
t_en: So you're saying that, instead of legendary heroes, you're more focused on commoners living on the peripheries of historical records?

t_ch: 或许是因为出身吧。我的祖先，<color=#dbc291ff>「阳雷骑士」塞涅俄丝，她高举长枪挑战天空，开创了延续至今的逐火时代</color>。
t_en: Perhaps I'm influenced by my background. My ancestor, <color=#dbc291ff>Seliose the Daythunder Knight, raised her spear against the Sky and ushered in the Flame-Chasing era that continues to this day.</color>

t_ch: 每一位天空后裔都听着她的故事长大。对于我们，塞涅俄丝的伟大毋庸质疑，但也因此…她伟岸的身影吞没了那个年代。
t_en: Her story is told to every child descended from the Skyfolk. We have no doubts about Seliose's greatness, yet... her towering figure overshadowed every record from that entire era.

t_ch: 阳雷骑士的亲人、盟友、伙伴，大战的幸存者、牺牲者…每当我在篝火边思念他们的故事，却总是发现传说的细节早已遗失，只留下塞涅俄丝的名字。
t_en: What about the Daythunder Knight's family, allies, and friends? What about the survivors and those who died in the great battles? Whenever I think about their stories by the campfire, I realize the details of those legends have been long lost... and only Seliose's name remains.

t_ch: 不知不觉中，她的光芒变得和艾格勒一样耀眼，盖过了地上万物……
t_en: Her brilliance had become as blinding as Aquila's, lording over everything on the ground.

t_ch: 所以，我很庆幸自己活在一个不缺少英雄的年代。
t_en: That's why... I'm glad that my era does not lack heroes.

t_ch: 先祖们只有悲伤的过去，但我有需要呵护的未来。我想做的，就是站在英雄们身后，在金色的浪潮将世界推向新生时，尽己所能疗愈每一个可能会溺亡的普通人。
t_en: All my ancestors had was a sad past, but I have a future I want to protect. Instead of becoming a hero and ushering in a golden deluge to baptize the world anew, I want to stay in the back line and save all the ordinary humans from drowning in the rising waves.

t_ch: 不必重回天空，而是在大地上和人们一同行走。这是我的愿望，我也相信只有这样，在创世的诗篇被写下的同时……
t_en: I wish not to return to the sky, but to walk among the masses on earth. I believe it's the only way for the saga of creation...

t_ch: 我才能为那空白的一页，添上凡人的注脚。
t_en: ...to include a footnote for the everyday people in its blank page.

t_ch: 嘟嘟…嘟？
t_en: Doot-Doot... Doot?

t_ch: 回想起自己一字一板的模样…还真让人难为情呀。
t_en: Ah... I'm getting embarrassed from remembering how I said all that.

t_ch: 不过，我的想法一直都没变哦。现在，我真的有机会站在英雄们身后，实现自己的理想了呀。
t_en: But my beliefs have never changed. Now I really have a chance to stay behind the heroes' frontline and realize my dream.

t_ch: …风堇？
t_en: ...Hyacine?

t_ch: 丹宝！你怎么在这里？
t_en: Dannie! What are you doing here?

t_ch: 没想到又在树庭见面了。白厄说你在为征伐艾格勒作准备，莫非……
t_en: I didn't expect to see you again in the Grove. Phainon mentioned you were gearing up for the campaign against Aquila. Are you here to...

t_ch: 猜对啦。要去往艾格勒的天上国度，需要天空一族的后人通过血脉仪式，召唤出先祖之灵，从他们那里获得祝福。
t_en: You guessed it. To reach Aquila's realm in the sky, descendants of the Sky people must perform a bloodline ceremony, summon the spirits of our ancestors, and get their blessings.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>只有这样，地上的生灵才能创造出通往天空的彩虹桥。</color>而我要寻访的第一位先祖，他的灵魂就驻留在树庭。
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>That's the only way for creatures on earth to forge the rainbow bridge to the sky</color>, and the soul of the first ancestor I want to see is here in the Grove.

t_ch: 为了取回{RUBY_B#天空之泰坦}艾格勒{RUBY_E#}的火种，建立彩虹桥是必需的。如此重要的仪式，你只身行动未免太过冒险。
t_en: The rainbow bridge is essential to retrieve the Coreflame of {RUBY_B#Sky Titan}Aquila{RUBY_E#}, but undertaking such a vital ceremony alone is far too risky.

t_ch: 放心吧，有小伊卡跟在我身边——它会保护好我的，对吧？
t_en: Don't worry. I have Little Ica with me. You'll protect me, won't you?

t_ch: 嘟！嘟嘟！
t_en: Doot! Doot-Doot!

t_ch: 乖~说起来，丹宝又在这里找什么呢？
t_en: Good Ica~ Well, what brings you here, Dannie?

t_ch: 我在尽己所能把和泰坦有关的神话和相关研究都记录下来。回到星穹列车以后，关于翁法罗斯的一切都会成为智库里的珍贵条目。
t_en: I'm collecting as many myths and research about the Titans as I can. Everything about Amphoreus is a valuable addition to our data bank when I get back to the Astral Express.

t_ch: 啊…对哦。如果一切顺利的话，这一役之后，丹宝和灰宝就能回家了吧？
t_en: Ah... right. If all goes as planned, you and Grayie will be able to go home after this mission, won't you?

t_ch: 嗯，前提是我们真的能破除「天外之界」的诅咒…尽管我有种预感，此事不会那么一帆风顺。
t_en: Yes, assuming we can actually break the curse about 'worlds beyond the sky'... I have a feeling it's not going to be smooth sailing.

t_ch: 嘿，难道丹宝现在也拥有了某种隐藏的预言之力？
t_en: Oh? Could it be that you, too, have some kind of hidden powers to make prophecies now?

t_ch: 那倒不是。只是之前和一位元老院议员的交流，让我有些怀疑这一切的真实性。
t_en: That's not it, but... I'm starting to question how real everything is after a conversation with a member of the Council of Elders.

t_ch: 嘟…嘟？
t_en: Doot... Doot?

t_ch: 我也和你一样没听懂呢，小伊卡。
t_en: Nope. I didn't understand that either, Little Ica.

t_ch: …别在意，有太多事我现在也无法理解。
t_en: ...Don't worry about it. There's a lot that I still don't understand.

t_ch: 既然已经碰巧见面了，丹宝要不要跟我一起来？
t_en: Since we've already met up, why don't you come with me, Dannie?
